questions.question: Why are Himalayan rivers called perennial rivers?
questions.answer: Because they receive water from both rainfall and melting snow throughout the year.

questions.question: Why do peninsular rivers have seasonal flow?
questions.answer: Because they depend mainly on rainfall for water supply.

questions.question: Why do Himalayan rivers form large deltas?
questions.answer: Because they carry huge amounts of silt and deposit it near their mouths.

questions.question: Why do rivers form meanders?
questions.answer: Because the slope of the land becomes gentle in the plains.

questions.question: Why does the Brahmaputra river cause floods every year?
questions.answer: Because it carries a large volume of water and silt during heavy rainfall.

questions.question: Why are peninsular rivers shorter than Himalayan rivers?
questions.answer: Because they originate from plateaus and have shorter courses.

questions.question: Why do most peninsular rivers flow eastwards?
questions.answer: Because the land slope of the plateau is towards the east.

questions.question: Why do Narmada and Tapi rivers flow westwards?
questions.answer: Because they flow through rift valleys created by faulting.

questions.question: Why are river valleys fertile?
questions.answer: Because rivers deposit rich alluvial soil.

questions.question: Why is the Sundarban delta important?
questions.answer: Because it is the largest delta in the world and supports rich biodiversity.

questions.question: Why is Godavari called the Dakshin Ganga?
questions.answer: Because it is the largest river of Peninsular India.

questions.question: Why are lakes important for river flow regulation?
questions.answer: Because they store water during heavy rainfall and release it gradually.

questions.question: Why are freshwater lakes mainly found in the Himalayan region?
questions.answer: Because many of them are formed by glacial activity.

questions.question: Why do lagoons form along the coast?
questions.answer: Because spits and bars block parts of coastal waters.

questions.question: Why are rivers important for human settlements?
questions.answer: Because they provide water and fertile land.

questions.question: Why is irrigation important in India?
questions.answer: Because agriculture depends heavily on water supply.

questions.question: Why is river water pollution increasing?
questions.answer: Because untreated sewage and industrial waste are discharged into rivers.

questions.question: Why was the Ganga Action Plan launched?
questions.answer: To reduce pollution and improve the water quality of the Ganga river.

questions.question: Why do rivers shift their channels frequently in floodplains?
questions.answer: Because of heavy silt deposition.

questions.question: Why do rivers form oxbow lakes?
questions.answer: Because of cut-offs formed during river meandering.

questions.question: Why are river islands formed in the Brahmaputra?
questions.answer: Because of large sediment deposition in its channel.

questions.question: Why do peninsular rivers form estuaries instead of deltas?
questions.answer: Because they carry less silt.

questions.question: Why is river transport important?
questions.answer: Because it is an economical mode of transportation.

questions.question: Why is water conservation important?
questions.answer: Because freshwater resources are limited.

questions.question: Why are rivers important for hydroelectric power?
questions.answer: Because flowing water can be used to generate electricity.
